project.programNotes: Real staging validation using provided reference floor-plan image. Generate three scenarios with existing-condition parsing, refined architectural plan output, achieved-vs-requested reporting, budget, and schedule.

scenarios.label: Light Refresh
scenarios.layoutDescription: This scenario prioritizes cost and speed, leveraging the existing layout to accommodate the team with minimal structural changes. New finishes, furniture, and paint create a fresh, modern aesthetic without altering the fundamental space plan. The layout works within existing wall lines, which may result in some compromises in ideal room sizes or adjacencies. Lower budget and shorter schedule reflect high reuse and minimal demolition.

schedulePhases.description: Updated furniture layout, finish selections, and minor partition modifications. Minimal permit requirements.
Remove existing finishes, flooring, and fixtures only. No structural or MEP demolition required.
New flooring, paint, lighting upgrades, and minor millwork. Reuse all existing walls and infrastructure.
Furniture installation, minor IT/AV updates, and signage.
Final walk-through, punch list items, and occupancy.

scenarios.aiSummary: This 'Light Refresh' scenario is ideal for the tenant seeking a quick, budget-friendly move-in. It efficiently reuses the existing infrastructure, making it the fastest and least expensive option. While it meets the basic program, the layout might not be perfectly optimized, potentially leading to a slightly lower efficiency score compared to more extensive renovations. Brokers can highlight the significant cost savings and expedited occupancy as key selling points, acknowledging that some minor layout compromises are inherent to this approach. Light Refresh achieves approximately 78% of the requested program while minimizing demolition, new partitions, and schedule impact. Remaining differences are fit variance, not a software failure.

scenarios.label: Moderate Build-Out
scenarios.layoutDescription: This plan strategically reconfigures key areas to align with the tenant's exact program, balancing cost and customization. Partial demolition allows for optimized flow and room adjacencies, while retaining some existing infrastructure to control expenses. The design focuses on creating a highly functional and collaborative environment tailored to professional services. Mid-range budget and schedule reflect selective demolition, retained core infrastructure, and targeted build-back.

scenarios.aiSummary: The 'Moderate Build-Out' offers a balanced approach, delivering a space closely matching the tenant's program without the full expense of a ground-up build. It involves selective demolition and new construction, resulting in a higher efficiency score and better functional layout than a 'Light Refresh.' While more costly and time-consuming than the first option, it provides a significantly improved long-term solution. Brokers can position this as the 'sweet spot' – achieving most program requirements with a reasonable budget and timeline, offering excellent value and operational efficiency for the professional services firm. Moderate Build-Out achieves approximately 92% of the requested program through targeted reconfiguration and selective reuse of existing conditions.

scenarios.label: Full Transformation
scenarios.layoutDescription: This scenario represents a complete reimagining of the space, starting from a base building shell to create an optimal, future-proof environment. The design is unconstrained by existing conditions, allowing for perfect alignment with the tenant's brand, workflow, and culture. It features bespoke solutions for collaboration, privacy, and amenities, maximizing both aesthetics and functionality. Highest budget and longest schedule reflect full interior redesign while preserving shell and fixed core elements.

schedulePhases.description: Complete architectural and engineering drawings, MEP design, structural review, and full permit package. Landlord approval required.
Complete gut demolition — all walls, ceilings, flooring, and MEP systems removed to base building condition.
New mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems installed from scratch. Includes HVAC, sprinkler, and data infrastructure.
Framing, drywall, specialty finishes, custom millwork, and all architectural elements.
Full furniture package, comprehensive technology infrastructure, audio-visual systems, and signage program.
Final inspections, commissioning, certificate of occupancy, and phased move-in.

scenarios.aiSummary: The 'Full Transformation' delivers the ultimate tailored solution, completely optimizing the 18,500 sq ft for the professional services firm's specific needs. This scenario boasts the highest efficiency score and provides the best long-term outcome, reflecting a premium investment in the tenant's workspace. While it entails the highest cost and longest timeline due to extensive demolition and rebuild, it eliminates all compromises. Brokers can emphasize the creation of a signature space that enhances brand identity, employee satisfaction, and operational excellence, justifying the higher upfront investment as a strategic long-term asset for the tenant. Full Transformation achieves approximately 100% of the requested program by preserving shell/core and redesigning flexible interior zones for best long-term fit.
